title: Unknown Protocol on OT VLAN Detection
app: TA for Zeek (Splunkbase 5466), Corelight App for Splunk (Splunkbase 3884)
spl:
index=ot sourcetype="zeek:conn:json" (like(vlan_name,"OT-%") OR cidrmatch("10.0.0.0/8",id.orig_h))
| eval svc=lower(service)
| where bytes_orig>0 AND bytes_resp>0
| where NOT (svc IN ("modbus","dnp3","bacnet","enip","s7comm","iec60870-5-104","hart-ip","dns","ntp") OR dest_port IN (502,20000,44818,47808,2222,2404,102,5094))
| stats sum(bytes_orig) as ob sum(bytes_resp) as rb dc(uid) as flows by "id.orig_h" "id.resp_h" dest_port proto service
| sort - flows
| head 200
description: Non-whitelisted protocols on ICS segments often indicate shadow IT, dual-homed misconfigurations, or tunneling that bypasses zone policies‚Äîeach can bridge IT threats into OT.
value: Non-whitelisted protocols on ICS segments often indicate shadow IT, dual-homed misconfigurations, or tunneling that bypasses zone policies‚Äîeach can bridge IT threats into OT.
implementation: Forward `conn.log` from Zeek on OT core taps with VLAN tags preserved. Maintain a Splunk lookup of approved services/ports per site; schedule nightly review of new triples (origin, destination, service). Tune DNS/NTP allowances.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: TA for Zeek (Splunkbase 5466), Corelight App for Splunk (Splunkbase 3884).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot` `sourcetype="zeek:conn:json"` (`service`, `proto`, `dest_port`, `vlan_id` or `vlan_name`).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Forward `conn.log` from Zeek on OT core taps with VLAN tags preserved. Maintain a Splunk lookup of approved services/ports per site; schedule nightly review of new triples (origin, destination, service). Tune DNS/NTP allowances.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="zeek:conn:json" (like(vlan_name,"OT-%") OR cidrmatch("10.0.0.0/8",id.orig_h))
| eval svc=lower(service)
| where bytes_orig>0 AND bytes_resp>0
| where NOT (svc IN ("modbus","dnp3","bacnet","enip","s7comm","iec60870-5-104","hart-ip","dns","ntp") OR dest_port IN (502,20000,44818,47808,2222,2404,102,5094))
| stats sum(bytes_orig) as ob sum(bytes_resp) as rb dc(uid) as flows by "id.orig_h" "id.resp_h" dest_port proto service
| sort - flows
| head 200
```

Understanding this SPL

**Unknown Protocol on OT VLAN Detection** ‚Äî Non-whitelisted protocols on ICS segments often indicate shadow IT, dual-homed misconfigurations, or tunneling that bypasses zone policies‚Äîeach can bridge IT threats into OT.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot` `sourcetype="zeek:conn:json"` (`service`, `proto`, `dest_port`, `vlan_id` or `vlan_name`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): TA for Zeek (Splunkbase 5466), Corelight App for Splunk (Splunkbase 3884). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot; **sourcetype**: zeek:conn:json. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=ot, sourcetype="zeek:conn:json". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **svc** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where bytes_orig>0 AND bytes_resp>0` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where NOT (svc IN ("modbus","dnp3","bacnet","enip","s7comm","iec60870-5-104","hart-ip","dns","ntp") OR dest_port IN (502,20‚Ä¶` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by "id.orig_h" "id.resp_h" dest_port proto service** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
‚Ä¢ Limits the number of rows with `head`.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (unexpected proto/port/service), Treemap (bytes by service), Timeline (first-seen connections).
visualization: Table (unexpected proto/port/service), Treemap (bytes by service), Timeline (first-seen connections).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
